questionHtml: Testis cells are eukaryotic cells. Identify the structures seen under the electron microscope in testis cells that are not present in prokaryotic cells.
markschemeHtml:
a. nucleus/nuclear membrane ✔

b. membrane bound organelles ✔

c. mitochondria ✔

d. rough ER/smooth ER/golgi apparatus ✔

e. lysosomes / centrioles ✔

f. large/80S ribosomes / ribosomes attached to a membrane ✔

g. linear chromosomes / histones ✔

meta.title: Testis cells are eukaryotic cells. Identify the structures seen under the electron microscope in testis cells that are not present in prokaryotic cells.
